---
title: "Wigner angles: SO(3) from Λ vs SU(2) from U"
engine: julia
format:
  html: default
---

InstructionalDecayTrees tracks each instruction path with a 4×4 Lorentz matrix
`Λ` (an SO(3,1) element in the `(px, py, pz, E)` basis) and a 2×2 SU(2)
matrix `U` that carries spinor phase information.

For **relative Wigner angles** between two decay paths, call the single public
API `wigner_zyz`. This notebook explains why a naive SO(3) decode of `Λ`
alone is not enough when `ψ` sits on a `2π` branch, and how the tracked SU(2)
matrix fixes that.

```{julia}
#| label: setup
using InstructionalDecayTrees
using FourVectors
using LinearAlgebra

const IDT = InstructionalDecayTrees
wrap2(a, b) = mod(a - b + π, 2π) - π
wrap4(a, b) = mod(a - b + 2π, 4π) - 2π
```

## Public API: `wigner_zyz`

After executing two instruction paths, form the relative tracker
`Δ = tracker_other * inv(tracker_reference)` and extract ZYZ Wigner angles:

```{julia}
#| label: path-comparison
p1 = FourVector(-0.1467, 0.2235, -0.7847; E = 2.0452)
p2 = FourVector(-0.0873, 0.1803, -0.5584; E = 0.7718)
p3 = FourVector(0.0056, -0.0349, 0.1413; E = 0.2017)
p4 = FourVector(0.2284, -0.3689, 1.2019; E = 2.2606)
objs = (p1, p2, p3, p4)

path_ref = (ToHelicityFrame((1, 2, 3)), ToHelicityFrameParticle2(3))
path_other = (
    ToHelicityFrameParticle2((2, 3, 4)),
    ToHelicityFrameParticle2((3, 4)),
    ToHelicityFrame((3,)),
)

cmp = compare_instruction_paths(path_ref, path_other, objs)
w = wigner_zyz(cmp.relative)
@assert abs(w.ϕ + 3.1181030111491173) < 5e-9
@assert abs(w.θ - 0.38580543795548133) < 5e-9
@assert abs(w.ψ - 9.42467967506533) < 5e-9
```

The relative transform here is a pure spatial rotation (`ξ ≈ 0`), which is the
regime where SU(2) branch information matters for Wigner-angle comparisons.

## Internal decoders (tutorial only)

The package also implements two **internal** decoders used below. They are not
exported; access them as `InstructionalDecayTrees._wigner_zyz_so3` and
`_wigner_zyz_su2` when studying the implementation.

| Decoder | Input | Branch of `ψ` |
|---------|-------|----------------|
| `_wigner_zyz_so3` | spatial block of `Λ` | `[-π, π)` via `normalize_psi` |
| `_wigner_zyz_su2` | `U` (pure rotation only) | `[-π, 3π)` from spinor phase |
| `wigner_zyz` | full helicity decode + SU(2) when `ξ ≈ 0` | matches physics/cross-check |

## SO(3): `Λ` loses the `2π` spinor branch

Build a pure ZYZ rotation and flip the sign of `U` (same `Λ`, opposite SU(2)
double cover):

```{julia}
#| label: spinor-sign-flip
ϕ0, θ0, ψ0 = 0.4, 1.1, 0.7
Λ = IDT._rz_xyze(ϕ0) * IDT._ry_xyze(θ0) * IDT._rz_xyze(ψ0)
U_plus = IDT._build_su2(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, ϕ0, θ0, ψ0)
U_minus = -U_plus

t_plus = LorentzTracker(Λ, U_plus)
t_minus = LorentzTracker(Λ, U_minus)

so3_plus = IDT._wigner_zyz_so3(t_plus)
so3_minus = IDT._wigner_zyz_so3(t_minus)
su2_plus = IDT._wigner_zyz_su2(t_plus)
su2_minus = IDT._wigner_zyz_su2(t_minus)
pub_plus = wigner_zyz(t_plus)
pub_minus = wigner_zyz(t_minus)

@assert so3_plus.ϕ ≈ so3_minus.ϕ
@assert so3_plus.θ ≈ so3_minus.θ
@assert so3_plus.ψ ≈ so3_minus.ψ   # SO(3) cannot see the sign flip

@assert abs(wrap4(su2_minus.ψ, su2_plus.ψ + 2π)) < 1e-12
@assert abs(wrap4(pub_minus.ψ, pub_plus.ψ + 2π)) < 1e-12
@assert pub_plus.ψ ≈ ψ0
@assert pub_minus.ψ ≈ ψ0 + 2π
```

SO(3) reports the same Euler triple for both trackers because `Λ` is
identical. SU(2) and the public `wigner_zyz` shift `ψ` by `2π` when `U → -U`,
which is required for strict agreement with the `decayangle` cross-check on
extended `ψ` intervals.

## Agreement on consistent branches

For a single SU(2) branch, SO(3), SU(2), and `wigner_zyz` agree modulo the
expected `2π` wraps:

```{julia}
#| label: branch-agreement
for (ϕ0, θ0, ψ0) in ((0.4, 1.1, 0.7), (-2.3, 0.9, 2.6), (1.9, 2.2, -1.4))
    Λ = IDT._rz_xyze(ϕ0) * IDT._ry_xyze(θ0) * IDT._rz_xyze(ψ0)
    U = IDT._build_su2(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, ϕ0, θ0, ψ0)
    t = LorentzTracker(Λ, U)
    a_so3 = IDT._wigner_zyz_so3(t)
    a_su2 = IDT._wigner_zyz_su2(t)
    a_pub = wigner_zyz(t)
    @assert abs(wrap2(a_so3.ϕ, a_su2.ϕ)) < 1e-12
    @assert abs(wrap2(a_so3.θ, a_su2.θ)) < 1e-12
    @assert abs(wrap4(a_so3.ψ, a_su2.ψ)) < 1e-12
    @assert abs(wrap4(a_pub.ψ, a_su2.ψ)) < 1e-12
end
```

## Takeaway

- Use **`wigner_zyz`** in application and cross-check code.
- Treat **`_wigner_zyz_so3`** / **`_wigner_zyz_su2`** as implementation
  details for understanding why both `Λ` and `U` are tracked.
